title: What An American thinks about Belgium

transcript.text: All right, what do I think about Belgium? I was in Belgium for like just a couple of days
I just rode from Maastricht to
Luxembourg passed through a couple of towns
Seemed like there is I mean I stayed in one like a house that was being constructed
So I guess there was construction going on at one place, but
Belgium is normal place, you know, they speak like three different languages. It's kind of like a mix between like French Dutch and
German maybe even
And I'm just kind of like in your old other test country
I
Was riding on the road and the car drivers have a different attitude towards bikes. They're like they'll drive like right up behind you
Like they're gonna hit you and but then they'll slow down like almost to a complete stop and then they'll get like way out in the other lane and pass you so
It was a cool way to I think they respected bikers. Well like in America when they pass you they don't slow down at all
And they'll give you your space before they come up on you
Um, but they don't give you as much space once they're passing you so
Um
And
Um
It's pretty crowded like lots of you know, not a whole it was kind of hard for me to find places to camp out and at one point
I camped out like on the side of some grassy field like right next to the road where every car could see me as they pass by
Because I had no choice. I was like any time. I had to do that
Because it was just like
You know
Lots of how it was just a pretty densely populated place
Um
Holland was densely populated too, but you could it was easy to camp out there and not be seen because there's so much foliage and
Stuff
So anyways, that's what I think about Belgium
